lenses.guidance: vv 44-45's 'be holy because I am holy' echoes at 1 Pet 1:16 as the rule for the church. Mk 7:19's 'thus he declared all foods clean' marks the canonical pivot — these laws are read across Scripture as fulfilled.

Food laws separate Israel from the nations. The covenant logic of vv 44-45 ties dietary practice to the redemptive arc — a holy people set apart so the salvation promise can travel through them to the world.

Clean/unclean categories foreshadow a deeper sorting. Acts 10:9-16 reverses the pattern when the sheet descends to Peter: the type-system gives way as he sees that no food, and no people, are unclean.
